description: Ouroboros nightly landscape corpus — 20 starred repos selected to benchmark agent systems, sovereign inference, context retrieval, orchestration, and market execution.

source.selection_rule: 5 tracks x 4 repos each; one repo per track per night; full cycle in 4 nights.

tracks.goal: Study agent loop design, tool surfaces, autonomy boundaries, and failure handling.
Study ingestion, code search, memory, and retrieval quality under real repo scale.
Study local-model serving, compression, cache layers, and deployment efficiency.
Study skills, hooks, multi-agent orchestration, and safe execution sandboxes.
Study forecasting, trading-agent structure, signing, and market-facing runtime constraints.

task_profiles.goal: Establish source-of-truth on what the repo actually does.
deliverables: One-sentence description grounded in primary files
Main extension points or operator surfaces

task_profiles.goal: Extract one mechanism CYNIC should consider and one CYNIC should reject.
deliverables: One adopt candidate with exact evidence
One reject candidate with exact evidence
Why the difference matters for CYNIC

task_profiles.goal: Map the external mechanism onto a concrete CYNIC surface.
deliverables: Estimated effort: S, M, or L

task_profiles.goal: Produce at most one bounded internal follow-up task from the repo analysis.

repos.why_it_matters: Directly relevant to Ouroboros because Hermes is the current external mutation engine.
repos.nightly_question: Which Hermes extension points should remain upstream-owned versus mirrored in CYNIC?

repos.why_it_matters: Representative of fast-moving open coding-agent ergonomics and operator expectations.
repos.nightly_question: What operator affordances make open coding agents feel faster without sacrificing auditability?

repos.why_it_matters: Strong baseline for edit loop reliability and repo comprehension under CLI constraints.
repos.nightly_question: Which parts of aider's edit loop are robust enough to emulate in Ouroboros?

repos.role: Agent toolkit and unified runtime stack
repos.why_it_matters: Good reference for multi-surface agent productization beyond a single CLI.
repos.nightly_question: Which runtime primitives deserve first-class treatment in CYNIC instead of living in scripts?

repos.role: Web search and scraping substrate for agents
repos.why_it_matters: Useful benchmark for external retrieval quality and operator-friendly web ingestion.
repos.nightly_question: What should CYNIC learn from Firecrawl's evidence collection pattern without becoming a SaaS wrapper?

repos.why_it_matters: Relevant to clean ingestion of PDFs, docs, and office files into agent-readable context.
repos.nightly_question: Where does CYNIC need stronger document normalization before retrieval or judgment?

repos.why_it_matters: Challenges the assumption that every retrieval problem needs embeddings first.
repos.nightly_question: Which parts of CYNIC retrieval should remain embedding-based and which should become structural?

repos.role: Code intelligence and MCP context server
repos.why_it_matters: Direct comparator for large-repo context serving and feature-graph search.
repos.nightly_question: What retrieval guarantees are missing from CYNIC MCP when navigating large codebases?

repos.why_it_matters: Reference point for sovereign serving breadth and operational packaging.
repos.nightly_question: What serving or deployment decisions should stay outside the kernel versus be abstracted behind Dogs?

repos.role: Training and local model operation stack
repos.why_it_matters: Relevant to future fine-tuning or specialization of Dogs.
repos.nightly_question: What data or evaluation pipeline would CYNIC need before any Dog fine-tuning is justified?

repos.why_it_matters: Compression is directly tied to local inference feasibility and latency budgets.
repos.nightly_question: Which compression gains would matter enough to change CYNIC routing or Dog roster composition?

repos.why_it_matters: Cache design is central to efficiency and repeated agent workflows.
repos.nightly_question: Where would cache layers actually reduce repeated work in CYNIC versus add opaque complexity?

repos.why_it_matters: Reference for agent HUDs, hooks, and teams-first augmentation.
repos.nightly_question: Which hook surfaces should Ouroboros expose explicitly instead of burying behavior in prompts?

repos.why_it_matters: Useful benchmark for reusable operational playbooks rather than single prompts.
repos.nightly_question: What makes a skill operationally reusable instead of becoming prompt ornament?

repos.why_it_matters: Direct comparator for multi-agent task decomposition and merge discipline.
repos.nightly_question: What should CYNIC measure before allowing broader sub-agent parallelism?

repos.why_it_matters: Safety and bounded execution are critical to trustworthy nightly autonomy.
repos.nightly_question: Which boundaries should CYNIC enforce at runtime rather than rely on policy text alone?

repos.why_it_matters: Represents forecasting-heavy work that is tempting but must be justified empirically.
repos.nightly_question: What evidence would be required before CYNIC adopts forecasting models instead of heuristics?

repos.why_it_matters: Good reference for role decomposition, strategy plurality, and market-facing evaluation.
repos.nightly_question: What parts of multi-strategy agent design survive contact with real verification requirements?

repos.why_it_matters: Signing and auth are where agent systems become real systems with blast radius.
repos.nightly_question: How should CYNIC separate judgment, authorization, and transaction execution?

repos.why_it_matters: Useful stress case for low-latency, risk-bearing, execution-adjacent design.
repos.nightly_question: Which CYNIC abstractions break first in low-latency market infrastructure contexts?
